name: schema-forward-compat
description: Two `overlay.set` events on the same wave: (a) a v1-shaped status overlay with NO schemaVersion field (the only shape any pre-#198 row has ever had), (b) the same status overlay rewritten by a hypothetical future kernel at schemaVersion=999. Replay must surface BOTH envelopes verbatim (the WS replay path is wire-faithful — issue #198 PR-A on read = strict, write = strict, replay = transparent). The accompanying test then sanity-checks `payload_schema_version()` reads `1` for (a) and `999` for (b), and that `filter_unsupported_overlay_versions` would drop (b) on the read path while passing (a) through. The fixture is the upgrade-stability anchor: regenerating it from a real session must keep both shapes round-trippable through `seed_events → WS replay → loader`.
